title: Verbalized Probabilities: Interpreting Language Models' Answers with Confidence

defines.name: verbalized probability
defines.definition: A method for training a language model to express epistemic uncertainty about its own answers in natural language confidence statements.

defines.name: CalibratedMath
defines.definition: A suite of elementary mathematics problems designed to evaluate calibration of model answers and their expressed confidence.

problem: Language models can answer questions well but often hallucinate, and existing calibration methods focus on token probabilities rather than calibrated uncertainty about the underlying claim.
